sections.title: One BalanceManager — shared inventory for both sides
sections.key_moment: `setupWithBalanceManager()` mints a *single* shared `BalanceManager` via `createAndShareBalanceManager()`, extracts its id from `effects.changedObjects` (filtered to the freshly-`Created` object whose `objectType` includes `BalanceManager`), and re-builds the client with `balanceManagers: { MANAGER_1: { address } }`. That one BM is the inventory wallet for *every* leg of the grid — the maker deposits both DEEP (to back asks) and SUI (to back bids) into it, and all bids and asks settle against the same account. There is no per-order coin splitting; the BM is the maker's book of record.

sections.title: Compute the grid — a two-sided ladder around mid
sections.key_moment: Reading `mid = await client.deepbook.midPrice(poolKey)` (wrapped in `withRetry` because the sandbox's `SimulateTransaction` occasionally returns without `commandResults`), then for each level `i` computing `off = (spreadBps / 10_000) * i`, `bid = round(mid * (1 - off))`, `ask = round(mid * (1 + off))`. `round` snaps every price down to the pool's `TICK` (0.000001) — an unrounded price is rejected by the pool. The grid is *symmetric* about mid: level 1 closest, level N furthest, a bid and an ask at each, so the bot quotes both sides and earns the round-trip spread.

sections.title: Rest the grid — POST_ONLY so the maker never takes
sections.key_moment: Each leg is placed with `client.deepbook.deepBook.placeLimitOrder({ ..., orderType: OrderType.POST_ONLY, payWithDeep: false })(tx)`, all batched into one PTB. `POST_ONLY` is the line that makes this a *maker*: the matching engine rejects (cancels) any leg that would immediately cross and fill, so the bot can never accidentally cross the spread and pay taker fees — it only ever *rests* liquidity. `payWithDeep: false` because the DEEP_SUI sandbox pool is whitelisted (no DEEP fee). The deposit-then-place is split across two `signAndExecute` calls so the BM is funded before the orders reference its balance.

sections.title: Stake DEEP — qualify for maker fee rebates
sections.key_moment: In a single PTB: `balanceManager.depositIntoManager(bmKey, 'DEEP', depositDeep)(tx)` then `governance.stake(poolKey, bmKey, amount)(tx)`. Staking DEEP through governance is what upgrades a plain maker into a *rebate-earning* maker — staked DEEP qualifies the account for maker fee rebates that accrue inside the same BM. The stake lands in `inactive_stake` this epoch and rolls into `active_stake` next epoch, so a correctness check must sum both (`info.active_stake + info.inactive_stake`).

sections.title: Unwind & re-quote — cancel-all, the smoke UI, and the live gate
sections.key_moment: `cancelAllOrders(poolKey, bmKey)(tx)` tears down every resting leg in one atomic call — the foundation of the maker's refresh loop: cancel the whole grid, re-read mid, re-quote. `listOpenOrders` (`accountOpenOrders`) is how the live gate proves the cycle: it asserts exactly `4` resting orders after quoting (2 bids + 2 asks) and `0` after cancel-all. Wiring `setupWithBalanceManagerBrowser()` into the smoke UI and running `pnpm vitest run` is the lesson's `final_verification`.
